D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Airmass & the Reddening Sun

drop the sun toward the horizon and watch the lengthening air path steal its blue

Airmass is the relative length of the path sunlight takes through the atmosphere. At the horizon it is roughly 38× the zenith path, so short-wavelength light is scattered away.

Lower the solar elevation and watch the sun disk redden as the airmass X climbs. The turbidity slider adds aerosol, deepening the effect. The right panel plots the transmitted spectrum.
